name: Acrobat Chocolate Polenta Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan over very low heat, melt chocolate and butter until melted and the mixture reaches body temperature.
2. Combine egg yolks, brown sugar, and white sugar in a bowl and stir until well mixed.
3. Add the melted chocolate to the yolk mixture and stir until combined.
4. Mix ground almonds, all-purpose flour, and cornmeal in a small bowl until well incorporated.
5. Pour the dry ingredients into the chocolate and egg mixture; stir until fully combined.
6. In a large bowl with an electric mixer, beat egg whites and cream of tartar until stiff peaks form.
7. Fold three portions of the beaten egg whites into the chocolate mixture using your hand as a paddle (push palm down to center, slide up side, turn over).
8. Do not over-mix; this prevents loss of air in egg whites, resulting in a denser texture.
9. Bake the cake in a buttered, floured 10-inch spring form pan at 400°F for 5 minutes.
10. Reduce heat to 350°F and bake for an additional 20-30 minutes or until fully cooked in the center.
11. Allow the cake to cool completely before removing from the pan.
